fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Hampshire Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Hampshire - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity new hampshire, how to transfer occupational therapist license to new hampshire, occupational therapist compact new hampshire, new hampshire occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: New Hampshire OPLC — Occupational Therapy
board.parent_dept: New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification (OPLC)
board.mailing_address:
Office of Professional Licensure and Certification
7 Eagle Square
Concord, NH 03301

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education per renewal period

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in New Hampshire.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Register for an account on the OPLC eLicensing portal (nh.us.thentiacloud.net/webs/nh/) and complete the online application for an Occupational Therapist license, paying the required application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, confirming graduation from an ACOTE-accredited occupational therapy program, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the OPLC.

steps.title: Provide NBCOT Verification
steps.description: Have your official NBCOT certification score report sent directly from the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) to the New Hampshire Board of Occupational Therapy Practice.

steps.title: Complete a Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the OPLC within the eLicensing portal to complete the required fingerprint-based criminal background check (CBC).

steps.title: Submit Additional Documentation
steps.description: Upload any other required documents, such as proof of citizenship/legal residency and the affidavit of eligibility, directly within the eLicensing portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Hampshire OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does New Hampshire have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: New Hampshire has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Hampshire while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in New Hampshire
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in New Hampshire, create an account and submit your application through the OPLC's eLicensing portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the OPLC eLicensing portal at nh.us.thentiacloud.net/webs/nh/, click 'Register here' to create a new user account. Once registered and logged in, select 'New Application' and choose the Occupational Therapist license type to begin your application.
